result:
Pre-registration complete and figcheck passes. Only `runs/pending_experiment.json` was created — no model or frozen files touched.

## What I designed this iteration

**Experiment 17: Nuisance-randomized training renders** — the first experiment in the `relighting` category in 16 iterations, and the family the frozen `pipeline/relight.py` docstring explicitly invites ("the loop may build a better trainable relighting for TRAINING data in model/").

**Why this target:** After three straight kept experiments (peak-commit decode, calibrated abstention, C4 rotation vote; 1857 → 1094.59 m), the metrics show night is the binding constraint everywhere: it's the worst bucket in all four areas, the primary metric *is* Frankfurt night, and night coverage has collapsed to 0.285–0.37 — barely above the 0.2 abstention floor.

**The diagnosed mechanism, with direct evidence:** Each (area, bucket) render is one seed-frozen realization of the sim's stochastic nuisances — sensor shot/read noise and the random thinning that picks which built-up pixels become lamps. I measured it on Prignitz: two seeds of the *night* render differ by mean |Δpx| 29/255 (79% of pixels differ by >8) against a render mean of only ~60/255 — roughly half the night image is realization-specific — while midday seeds differ by just 6/255. The 1024-way memorization head keys cell templates on the easiest discriminative signal, which at night SNR≈1 is that frozen texture; held-out eval locations carry unseen realizations, so templates misfire, fields go diffuse, and the model abstains or misses by ~1 km.

**The one change (train.py only):** each bucket's 6,000 training crops are drawn ⅓ from the stored eval-matched PNG and ⅓ each from two fresh-seed re-renders of `reference.tif` through the untouched frozen `relight()` function (~19 s/render measured, +4 min/area). Model, losses, decode, calibration, CLI, and export are all unchanged, so gates stay at ~3.3 MiB / ~2.7 ms and the downside is bounded near current best.

**Prediction:** the physics concentrates the effect where the problem is — night medians drop 10–25% and night coverage rises toward ≥0.4, worst-case median 880–1010 m (from 1094.59 m); ~flat if realization overfit isn't what the head exploits.

The harness can now run the implementation, training, and scoring stages.

tool_input.command: ls runs/20260721_141132_iter4/ 2>/dev/null; ls runs/20260721_133421_iter3/ 2>/dev/null; cat /FROZEN 2>/dev/null; ls
tool_input.description: List run dirs and FROZEN manifest

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite ".schema experiments" && sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT length(architecture_svg) FROM experiments WHERE id=16;" 2>/dev/null; sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT architecture_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=16;" > /tmp/exp16.svg 2>/dev/null; wc -c /tmp/exp16.svg
tool_input.description: Check DB schema and dump exp16 SVG

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=16;" > /tmp/exp16.svg && head -c 3000 /tmp/exp16.svg
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=16;" | tee /tmp/exp16.svg | head -c 6000
tool_input.description: Dump exp 16 architecture SVG to file and preview

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python -c "
import cairosvg
cairosvg.svg2png(url='/tmp/newfig.svg', write_to='/tmp/newfig.png', output_width=1470)
print('ok')
" 2>/dev/null || (command -v rsvg-convert && rsvg-convert -w 1470 /tmp/newfig.svg -o /tmp/newfig.png && echo ok) || (command -v inkscape && echo have-inkscape)
tool_input.description: Render SVG to PNG for visual check

tool_input.command: command -v rsvg-convert convert inkscape chromium google-chrome; .venv/bin/python -c "import PIL; print('pil ok')"

.venv/bin/python - <<'PYEOF'
import json
from pathlib import Path

svg = Path("/tmp/newfig.svg").read_text()

exp = {
  "title": "Nuisance-randomized training renders: each bucket's training crops drawn from three seeded realizations of the frozen relighting sim",
  "category": "relighting",
  "hypothesis": (
    "Night is now the binding constraint everywhere: it is the worst lighting bucket in all four development areas, "
    "the primary metric IS Frankfurt night (1094.59 m), and night coverage has collapsed toward the abstention floor "
    "(0.285-0.37 vs 0.46-0.71 in bright buckets) — the confidence head is already throwing away two thirds of night "
    "crops and the survivors still miss by ~1 km. The cause this experiment attacks: each (area, bucket) training/eval "
    "image is ONE frozen realization of the relighting sim's stochastic nuisances — the seeded shot/read sensor noise "
    "and the seeded random thinning that decides which built-up pixels become lamps. Measured directly on prignitz: two "
    "different seeds of the night render differ by mean |dpx| 29/255 (79% of pixels differ by >8) against a render mean "
    "of only ~60/255 — roughly HALF the night image is realization-specific nuisance — while midday differs by just "
    "6/255. Training crops therefore show each location with a fixed noise/lamp texture for all epochs, and a 1024-way "
    "location-memorization head keys each cell's template on the easiest discriminative signal available, which at "
    "night SNR~1 is precisely that frozen texture. Held-out eval locations carry unseen realizations of the same "
    "nuisances, so the learned templates misfire, fields go diffuse, and the model abstains to the floor or commits "
    "wrongly — exactly the observed night signature. Sampling each bucket's training crops from THREE seeded "
    "realizations of the frozen sim (the stored eval-matched render plus two fresh-seed re-renders of reference.tif "
    "through the untouched pipeline.relight.relight) makes realization texture non-discriminative between locations, "
    "forcing templates onto the seed-stable structure (roads, building masses, where the built-up glow sits) — which "
    "is exactly what eval crops share with training crops. This is the first experiment in the relighting family in 16 "
    "iterations, and it is the training-side relighting that pipeline/relight.py's own NOTE explicitly sanctions; the "
    "eval set is untouched."
  ),
  "method": (
    "model/train.py only. load_training_tensors() partitions each bucket's rng.choice picks into three near-equal "
    "parts: part 0 is extracted from the stored data/<area>/relight/<bucket>.png (seed_0 — exactly what eval sees); "
    "parts 1 and 2 are extracted from relight(ref, LIGHTING_BUCKETS[bucket], meta['gsd_m'], "
    "stable_hash(f'{area}:{bucket}:trainreal:{r}')) for r=1,2, rendered on the fly from the area's reference.tif via "
    "the frozen pipeline.relight.relight function (imported, never modified), holding at most one full raster + the "
    "reference in memory at a time (~19 s/render measured, +12 renders = ~4 min/area). Everything else — model.py, "
    "losses, decode, C4 vote, conf calibration on the stored PNGs, CLI, ONNX export — unchanged."
  ),
  "expected_outcome": (
    "The effect is concentrated in dark buckets by construction (seed-to-seed render difference is ~5x smaller at "
    "midday than at night), so bright cells should move little. If realization overfit is binding at night: night "
    "medians (835-1095 m across areas) drop 10-25% and night coverage rises from 0.285-0.37 toward >=0.4, giving a "
    "predicted primary worst-case median of 880-1010 m (from 1094.59 m); since all four night cells sit within 260 m "
    "of the worst, the improvement must be broad, not single-cell. Null: the head is not keying on realization "
    "texture -> metric ~flat (+/-3%); downside is bounded near current best because a third of training data remains "
    "exactly eval-matched and the model/loss/decode are unchanged. Gates unchanged (3302661 bytes, ~2.7 ms host "
    "proxy); training wall time +~4 min/area."
  ),
  "init_strategy": "pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (torchvision, BSD-3)",
  "eli5": (
    "The model learns night navigation from simulated night photos, and the simulator rolls dice for two things: the "
    "grainy sensor static, and which streetlights and lit windows happen to be on. Until now, every practice photo of "
    "a place used one single dice roll, frozen forever — so the model could ace practice by memorizing the static, "
    "like recognizing a photocopy by its smudges instead of reading it. On new places the smudges are different, and "
    "it gets lost exactly where the picture is mostly smudge: at night. Now the practice photos come from three "
    "different dice rolls of the same city, so the only thing worth memorizing is what is actually there — the roads, "
    "the buildings, and where the lit areas sit."
  ),
  "architecture": {"stages": [
    {"name": "Camera frame", "detail": "128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Rotation fan", "detail": "the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Feature extractor", "detail": "ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Layout summary", "detail": "a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Lighting gate", "detail": "a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Probability map", "detail": "two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses; the four resulting heat maps are averaged into one voted map — a location only stays hot if it wins from every direction", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Decode", "detail": "the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Confidence", "detail": "the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Output", "detail": "(lat, lon, confidence)", "changed": False},
    {"name": "Training signal", "detail": "unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before", "changed": False, "train_only": True},
    {"name": "Training data", "detail": "each practice image now comes from one of three independently rolled versions of the lighting simulator — same city, same physics, but different random sensor static and a different random choice of which streetlights/windows are lit — so memorizing one roll's grain and lamp pattern no longer pays off", "changed": True, "train_only": True}
  ]},
  "architecture_svg": svg,
  "implementation_brief": (
    "Edit ONLY model/train.py. model/model.py and everything under pipeline/ are untouched.\n"
    "\n"
    "1. Imports: add `import rasterio` and `from pipeline.relight import relight`; extend the existing "
    "pipeline.common import with `stable_hash` (LIGHTING_BUCKETS, area_dir, load_meta are already imported). "
    "pipeline/relight.py is frozen but its NOTE explicitly permits model/ importing it for training data; do not "
    "modify it.\n"
    "\n"
    "2. New module-level constant near the existing ones: `TRAIN_REALIZATIONS = 3`.\n"
    "\n"
    "3. Rewrite load_training_tensors(area, data_dir, max_crops_per_bucket, rng) as follows, keeping its signature "
    "and return contract (uint8 tensor N×H×W×3, float32 targets N×2) identical:\n"
    "   - meta = load_meta(...); crops = list_crops(...) exactly as now.\n"
    "   - Open the daytime reference once per area:\n"
    "       with rasterio.open(area_dir(area, data_dir) / 'reference.tif') as src:\n"
    "           ref = src.read().transpose(1, 2, 0)   # HxWx3 uint8\n"
    "   - For each bucket in LIGHTING_BUCKETS (keep dict order):\n"
    "       picks = rng.choice(len(crops), size=min(max_crops_per_bucket, len(crops)), replace=False)  # unchanged\n"
    "       for r in range(TRAIN_REALIZATIONS):\n"
    "           part = picks[r::TRAIN_REALIZATIONS]\n"
    "           if r == 0:\n"
    "               img = np.asarray(Image.open(area_dir(area, data_dir) / 'relight' / f'{bucket}.png'))\n"
    "           else:\n"
    "               img = relight(ref, LIGHTING_BUCKETS[bucket], meta['gsd_m'],\n"
    "                             stable_hash(f'{area}:{bucket}:trainreal:{r}'))\n"
    "           for i in part:\n"
    "               c = crops[i]\n"
    "               angle = float(rng.uniform(0, 360))\n"
    "               xs.append(extract_crop(img, c['cx'], c['cy'], angle))\n"
    "               ys.append(crop_center_norm(meta, c['cx'], c['cy']))\n"
    "           del img   # keep at most one full render + ref in memory\n"
    "     After the bucket loop, `del ref`. Note the per-crop rng draw order changes vs. the old code (angles are "
    "drawn part-by-part); that is acceptable — determinism under --seed is what matters, exact sample reproduction "
    "is not.\n"
    "\n"
    "4. calibrate_conf_shift is UNTOUCHED — it must keep reading the stored relight PNGs so conf_shift is calibrated "
    "on eval-matched appearance.\n"
    "\n"
    "5. train_area, the epoch loop, param groups/LRs, seeds, and export are unchanged. Add one key to the returned "
    "info dict: \"train_realizations\": TRAIN_REALIZATIONS.\n"
    "\n"
    "6. Contracts to preserve (frozen scorer depends on them): CLI stays exactly "
    "`python -m model.train --area <a> --out-dir <dir> [--data-dir D] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] "
    "[--seed N]`; one ONNX per area exported to <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx via model.py's export_onnx, taking "
    "1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1] and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1]; "
    "exported ONNX <= 4 MiB and host latency proxy <= 250 ms (nothing in this change affects the graph, so both are "
    "unchanged at ~3.3 MiB / ~2.7 ms).\n"
    "\n"
    "7. Cost note: each fresh render of a ~7000² raster takes ~19 s (measured); 2 fresh seeds × 6 buckets = 12 "
    "renders ≈ +4 min per area. Peak extra memory is one ~150 MB render plus the ~150 MB reference."
  )
}

Path("runs/pending_experiment.json").write_text(json.dumps(exp, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
print("written", len(json.dumps(exp)))
PYEOF
